title: Google Moves Q Day Deadline to 2029
summary: Google has bumped up its Q Day deadline to 2029, warning the industry to move off RSA and EC quickly. This deadline is far sooner than previously thought.
body:
Google has announced that it is moving its Q Day deadline to 2029, a significant shift from previous estimates. This change in timeline is a warning to the entire industry to accelerate its transition away from RSA and EC encryption methods. 

 The move is driven by the rapid advancements in quantum computing, which threaten to render current encryption standards obsolete. Google's updated deadline underscores the urgency for companies to adopt quantum-resistant encryption methods to protect their data and systems. 

 As the industry reacts to Google's new deadline, experts predict a surge in investment and research into post-quantum cryptography. The race is on to develop and implement secure encryption solutions before the anticipated arrival of quantum computers capable of breaking current encryption standards. Companies that fail to adapt risk exposing their data to potential quantum attacks, making Google's Q Day deadline a critical milestone for the industry's cybersecurity landscape.
twitterThread: 🚨 Google just moved its Q Day deadline to 2029! 🚨
Here's why this matters: RSA and EC encryption won't be secure against quantum computers. Google's warning the entire industry to move off these standards ASAP.
The wild part: this deadline is years sooner than expected. Companies need to accelerate their transition to quantum-resistant encryption methods, or risk being left behind.
Let that sink in: by 2029, your data could be vulnerable to quantum attacks if you're still using RSA or EC. Google's Q Day deadline is a wake-up call for the industry.
This changes everything for cybersecurity. Get ready for a surge in investment and research into post-quantum cryptography.
standaloneTweet: Google's Q Day deadline just got a lot closer - 2029 is the new target. Companies must ditch RSA and EC encryption ASAP or risk quantum attacks. What's your plan? https://ainformed.dev/articles/Google-Moves-Q-Day-Deadline-to-2029
